title: Writing a register array does not work
body:
## How to reproduce

The setup: I have a memory with a read and write port, `reg [31:0] mem [0:3]`. A simple counter will serve us as address. The memory is initialized to all `0xAAAA` and we want to write it all to `0xBBBB`. Here's the Verilog code (The contents of `mem_reset.bin` is all `0xAAAA`, you can imagine):

```verilog
module MulAccVerifier (
  input               reset,
  input               clk
);
  reg [2:0] counter;
  reg [31:0] mem [0:3];

  /* Init handling */
  always @(posedge clk) begin
    if($initstate()) begin
      assume(reset);
    end
  end
  initial begin
    $readmemb("mem_reset.bin",mem);
  end

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    if(!reset) begin
      if(counter == 3'b111) begin
        /* Fail on purpose for debugging */
        assert(1'b0);
        assert((mem[counter[1:0]] == 32'hBBBB));
      end
    end
  end

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    if(reset) begin
      counter <= 3'b000;
    end else begin
      counter <= (counter + 3'd001);
      mem[counter[1:0]] <= 32'hBBBB;
    end
  end
endmodule
```

## Actual behavior

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14054505/132772338-b32720fd-6905-41d2-88ca-0a15aacbc1b0.png)

The writes are very sporadic. They may be cycles late, and more often than not only one of the values is written at all. This is really non-deterministic based on what the solver produces as counter-example (well each run gives the same result, but the result may vary if the net list changes), and I couldn't really figure out a pattern.

Note that I am running this through SymbiYosys because this initially came up during formal tests and the issue didn't appear in my simulation (I think it's using Verilator, but not sure). The `.sby` file is very simple, it sets `mode bmc`, `depth 15`, `[engines] smtbmc` and reads the verilog and the bin file.

## Expected behavior

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14054505/132772638-2326c953-fcf6-414f-aee4-280dbe6d4fab.png)

I got this behavior by replacing the `mem[counter[1:0]] <= 32'hBBBB;` line in the above snippet with

```verilog
      int i;
      for (i = 0; i < 4; i = i + 1) begin
        if (counter[1:0] == i) begin
          mem[i] <= 32'hBBBB;
        end
      end
```

With that change, Verilog prints `Warning: Replacing memory \mem with list of registers.` which I think may be relevant to locate the root source of the bug.

I did not check in-depth for issue duplicates, but this may be related to #2570. My `yosys` version is `Yosys 0.9+4052 (git sha1 687f381b69, gcc 10.3.0 -fPIC -Os)`, I will try again with master tomorrow. `symbiyosys` is from master.
